name: Software Development
description: Two-year diploma focused on full-stack development, mobile applications, and database design. Hands-on training using industry-standard tools and frameworks with real-world projects.

tuition.note: per year, does not include textbooks or supplies

domestic: Math 30-1 or Math 30-2 or Pure Math 30 or Applied Math 30 (50% minimum)
international: High school diploma or international equivalent (12 years education)
English proficiency: IELTS 6.0 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 80

name: Bachelor of Technology - Software Development
description: Two-year degree program( after diploma) designed to accelerate careers in full-stack development. Students design, develop and delpoy software solutions using recent technologies, advanced design patterns, algorithms and data structures. Builds on existing IT diploma with focus on programming, web development, database design, software testing, and security.

tuition.note: per year, does not include textbooks or supplies

prerequisites.note: Non-direct entry degree. Requires completion of IT diploma before admission.
domestic: SAIT Information Technology diploma OR equvalent from accredited institution
Eligible diplomas include: SAIT Software Development diploma, SAIT Information Technology - Software Development major
international: SAIT Information Technology diploma OR equvalent from accredited institution
English proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 80
Credential evaluation may be required for non-SAIT diplomas

additionalInfo.applicationDeadline: June 30 (Fall, domestic), May 29 (Fall, international), October 23 (Winter, domestic), October 9 (Winter, international)
additionalInfo.totalProgramLength: 4 years total (2 years diploma + 2 years degree)
additionalInfo.specialNotes: Degree completion program for IT diploma graduates. Multiple start dates per year. Optional co-op workplacement available.
additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Accepts graduates from SAIT Software Development diploma and IT - Software Development major diploma
additionalInfo.transferNote: Transfer agreements available with Alberta institutions. Visit https://transferalberta.alberta.ca/transfer-alberta-search/#/audienceTypeStep or see program page for details.

name: Accounting
description: One-year certificate program equipping students with essential knowledge and skills for accounting, finance, and business management roles. Covers accounting fundamentals including financial statements, accounts receivable/payable, full-cycle accounting, and leveraging information technology. Culminates in work-integrated learning capstone project.

tuition.note: total program cost (can be completed over up to 5 years), does not include books and supplies

international: High school diploma or international equivalent
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.0 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 80 (verify exact requirements)
Math equivalency to Alberta Math 30-1 or 30-2

additionalInfo.applicationDeadline: Rolling admissions (April 30 for Spring, June 30 for Fall, October 23 for Winter)
additionalInfo.specialNotes: Small class sizes (max 40 students). Includes capstone work-integrated learning project. Can be completed part-time or full-time. Transfer pathway to SAIT diploma and degree programs.

name: Business Administration - Accounting Major
description: Two-year diploma building practical and fundamental accounting skills with focus on analyzing company financial performance. Students learn accounting principles in real business contexts, financial statement preparation, tax calculation, financial concepts application, and management information systems. Prepares students for Certified Public Accountant Advanced Certificate.

tuition.note: per year, does not include books and supplies

international: High school diploma or international equivalent (12 years education)
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.0 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 80 (verify exact requirements)
Math equivalency to Alberta Math 30-1 or 30-2

additionalInfo.applicationDeadline: Rolling admissions (April 30 for Spring, June 30 for Fall, October 23 for Winter)
additionalInfo.specialNotes: Students declare major before second year. CPA Advanced Certificate preparation included. Transfer agreements available - see https://www.transferalberta.ca. Can transfer to Athabasca University Bachelor of Management or Griffith University Bachelor of Commerce.

name: Bachelor of Business Administration - Accounting Major
description: Four-year business degree focused on measuring financial performance, advanced accounting topics, and quantitative skills. Includes courses required to enter the Chartered Professional Accountant (CPA) Professional Education Program. Blends management and financial accounting concepts with emphasis on ethics, financial reporting, tax regulations, and management information systems.

tuition.note: per year (2025/26 estimate), does not include books and supplies

domestic: Two courses from Group A (academics): Accounting, Biology, Business, Chemistry, Economics, Finance, Geography, History, IT, Languages, Marketing, Math 31, Philosophy, Physics, Political Science, Psychology, Science, Social Studies 30-1, Sociology
One additional course from Group A or Group B (Art, Drama, Music, PE, Religion, Social Studies 30-2)
international: High school diploma or international equivalent (12 years education)
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 86 (verify exact requirements)
Math equivalency to Alberta Math 30-1 or 30-2
alternativePathways: SAIT Business Administration diploma with minimum 2.3 GPA (67%)
Two-year diploma or bachelor's degree with 2.3 GPA plus English 30-1 and Math 30-1/30-2 equivalents

additionalInfo.cpaCertification: Includes courses required for CPA Professional Education Program
additionalInfo.specialNotes: First semester common for all majors. Students declare major in second semester through competitive screening process. Transfer agreements available with Lethbridge College, Portage College, Northwestern Polytechnic, Bow Valley College - see https://www.transferalberta.ca. Graduates can pursue MBA or MPAcc at University of Saskatchewan, Colorado State University, or Vancouver Island University.

name: Bachelor of Science Construction Project Management
description: Canada's only four-year undergraduate science degree in construction management. Blends practical skill sets with foundational and advanced concepts in construction technology and project management best practices. Curriculum aligns with residential, commercial, industrial, and infrastructure industry needs. Includes internship for real-world application and networking with industry leaders.

tuition.note: per year (average), does not include books and supplies

domestic: Two courses from Group A: Math 31, Biology 30, Chemistry 30, Physics 30, Science 30
One course from Group A or Group B (Art, Drama, Music, PE, Religion, Social Studies, Languages)
international: High school diploma or international equivalent (12 years education)
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 86 (verify exact requirements)
alternativePathways: Two-year diploma or bachelor's degree with 2.3 GPA (67%) plus English 30-1 and Math 30-1 or Math 30-2

additionalInfo.specialNotes: Canada's only 4-year undergraduate construction management degree. Includes mandatory internship. Transfer options to University of Victoria MBA, University of Calgary Haskayne MBA/Master of Management. Transfer agreements available - see https://www.transferalberta.ca

name: Mechanical Engineering Technology
description: Two-year hands-on diploma program providing technical, analytical and problem-solving skills for careers in mechanical engineering. Foundation in core engineering principles combined with practical application and design. Students choose from three specializations in Year 2: Design and Analysis (mechanical systems, vibrations, thermodynamics), Design and Automation (automated systems, PLCs, robotics), or Design and Development (prototyping, rapid prototyping, ergonomic design).

tuition.note: per year, does not include books and supplies

domestic: Physics 20 and Chemistry 20 (60% each) OR Science 30 (60% minimum)
international: High school diploma or international equivalent (12 years education)
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.0 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 80 (verify exact requirements)
Math equivalency to Alberta Math 30-1 or 30-2

specializations: Design and Analysis (mechanical systems, vibrations, thermodynamics)
Design and Automation (PLCs, robotics, automated systems)
Design and Development (prototyping, rapid prototyping, ergonomics)
additionalInfo.specialNotes: Students declare specialization before Year 2 based on academic performance and preference. Transfer agreements available with Lakehead University (BEng Mechanical), UBC (BASc Mechanical), University of Calgary (BSc Energy Engineering), and others - see https://www.transferalberta.ca

name: Electrical Engineering Technology
description: Two to three-year diploma program providing diverse skill set in electrical engineering for careers in renewable and conventional energy. Foundation for lifelong learning in rapidly evolving electrical engineering technologies. Covers electricity and power systems, electrical circuit design, automation and control systems, with hands-on labs simulating real-world challenges. Includes project management, industry standards compliance, and professional skills training.

tuition.note: per year (average for 2-year program), does not include books and supplies

international: High school diploma or international equivalent (12 years education)
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.0 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 80 (verify exact requirements)
Math equivalency to Alberta Math 30-1 or 30-2

additionalInfo.specialNotes: Flexible learning options: complete in 2 years full-time (daytime) or 3 years part-time (blended evenings/weekends). Co-op placements and industry-sponsored projects available. Transfer agreements with UVic/Camosun (BEng Electrical/Computer Engineering), Thompson Rivers University - see https://www.transferalberta.ca

name: Bachelor of Business Administration - Financial Services Major
description: Four-year business degree for careers in financial advising, banking, investing, and insurance. Provides essential skills and industry certifications including Investment Funds in Canada (IFIC) and Canadian Securities Course (CSC). Students learn to analyze client needs, provide tailored financial solutions, master relationship selling, and uphold ethical and regulatory standards in the financial services industry.

tuition.note: per year (2025/26 estimate), does not include books and supplies

domestic: Two courses from Group A (academics): Accounting, Biology, Business, Chemistry, Economics, Finance, Geography, History, IT, Languages, Marketing, Math 31, Philosophy, Physics, Political Science, Psychology, Science, Social Studies 30-1, Sociology
One additional course from Group A or Group B (Art, Drama, Music, PE, Religion, Social Studies 30-2)
international: High school diploma or international equivalent (12 years education)
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 86 (verify exact requirements)
Math equivalency to Alberta Math 30-1 or 30-2
alternativePathways: SAIT Business Administration diploma with minimum 2.3 GPA (67%)
Two-year diploma or bachelor's degree with 2.3 GPA plus English 30-1 and Math 30-1/30-2 equivalents

additionalInfo.specialNotes: First semester common for all majors. Students declare major in second semester through competitive screening process. Includes industry-recognized certifications (IFIC, CSC). Transfer agreements available with Bow Valley College - see https://www.transferalberta.ca

name: Bachelor of Business Administration - Human Resource Management Major
description: Four-year business degree accredited by Human Resources Institute of Alberta (HRIA) preparing students for careers in human resources management. Covers HR theory and practice, employment law, leadership, health and safety programs, compensation, skills development, and employment relationships. Graduates receive Certified Professional in Human Resources (CPHR) designation upon completion.

tuition.note: per year (2025/26 estimate), does not include books and supplies

domestic: Two courses from Group A (academics): Accounting, Biology, Business, Chemistry, Economics, Finance, Geography, History, IT, Languages, Marketing, Math 31, Philosophy, Physics, Political Science, Psychology, Science, Social Studies 30-1, Sociology
One additional course from Group A or Group B (Art, Drama, Music, PE, Religion, Social Studies 30-2)
international: High school diploma or international equivalent (12 years education)
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 86 (verify exact requirements)
Math equivalency to Alberta Math 30-1 or 30-2
alternativePathways: SAIT Business Administration diploma with minimum 2.3 GPA (67%)
Two-year diploma or bachelor's degree with 2.3 GPA plus English 30-1 and Math 30-1/30-2 equivalents

additionalInfo.professionalDesignation: CPHR (Certified Professional in Human Resources) - included with graduation
additionalInfo.accreditation: Accredited by Human Resources Institute of Alberta (HRIA)
additionalInfo.specialNotes: First semester common for all majors. Students declare major in second semester through competitive screening process. Graduates earn CPHR designation upon completion. Transfer agreements available with Lethbridge College, Portage College, Bow Valley College - see https://www.transferalberta.ca

name: Business Administration - Financial Services Major
description: Two-year diploma preparing students for careers in financial services including financial planning, investment advising, insurance, trust management, and compliance. Students learn Canadian financial system, relationship selling, client needs assessment, financial planning concepts, and industry regulations. Includes preparation for Investment Funds in Canada (IFIC) exam.

tuition.note: per year, does not include books and supplies

international: High school diploma or international equivalent (12 years education)
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.0 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 80 (verify exact requirements)
Math equivalency to Alberta Math 30-1 or 30-2

additionalInfo.applicationDeadline: Rolling admissions (April 30 for Spring, June 30 for Fall, October 23 for Winter)
additionalInfo.examPreparation: IFIC (Investment Funds in Canada) exam preparation included
additionalInfo.specialNotes: Students declare major before second year. IFIC exam prep included. Transfer pathway to Griffith University Bachelor of Commerce in Finance. Transfer agreements available - see https://www.transferalberta.ca

name: Business Administration - Human Resource Management Major
description: Two-year diploma preparing students for careers in HR including recruitment, training, payroll, and HR management. Students learn HR management practices, employment law and regulations, leadership skills, and HR data analysis. Covers essential HR tasks in real-world business contexts with practical exercises and scenarios.

tuition.note: per year, does not include books and supplies

international: High school diploma or international equivalent (12 years education)
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.0 (no band below 5.5) OR TOEFL iBT 80 (verify exact requirements)
Math equivalency to Alberta Math 30-1 or 30-2

additionalInfo.applicationDeadline: Rolling admissions (April 30 for Spring, June 30 for Fall, October 23 for Winter)
additionalInfo.specialNotes: Students declare major before second year. Transfer pathway to Griffith University Bachelor of Business. Transfer agreements available - see https://www.transferalberta.ca

name: Software Development
description: Two-year diploma program providing hands-on training in full-stack web development, AI, data science, and DevOps. Part of the Digital Media and IT (DMIT) stream, focusing on practical industry skills.

tuition.note: Estimated first-year tuition; excludes mandatory fees and books

international: High school diploma or international equivalent
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.0) OR TOEFL iBT 80 (no section below 20)
Math equivalency to Alberta Math 30-1 or 30-2
Credential evaluation (IQAS or equivalent) may be required

additionalInfo.specialNotes: Requires a laptop (BYOD). Highly competitive program with emphasis on project-based learning.
additionalInfo.transferNote: Up to 50% of program requirements can be satisfied via transfer credit. Minimum 80% curriculum match required.
additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Graduates are eligible to ladder into BAIST - Information Systems or BTech in Management.

name: Bachelor of Applied Information Systems Technology - Information Systems
description: A two-year degree completion program (following a diploma) designed to bridge technical coding skills with business strategy. Focuses on enterprise-level software development and systems planning.
credentials: Bachelor of Applied Information Systems Technology (BAIST)

tuition.note: Per year; based on standard course load

domestic: Completion of an IT-related diploma (e.g., Software Development)
international: Recognized IT diploma or equivalent 60-credit post-secondary credential
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.0) OR TOEFL iBT 80
Minimum GPA equivalent of 2.3 on a 4.0 scale

additionalInfo.specialNotes: Includes a mandatory 4-month capstone project or work term.
additionalInfo.transferNote: Accepts graduates from recognized IT diplomas across Canada. Visit https://transferalberta.alberta.ca/
additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Direct laddering from NAIT Software Development diploma.

name: Business Administration - Accounting
description: A two-year diploma program providing a solid foundation in financial theory and technical accounting expertise. Students master manual accounting techniques and current industry software through hands-on learning.

tuition.note: Estimated first-year tuition; excludes books and mandatory fees

domestic: Overall minimum average of 65% in the five required courses
international: High school diploma or international equivalent
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.0 (no band below 6.0) OR TOEFL iBT 70 (no section below 17)
Math equivalency to Alberta Math 30-1 or 30-2

additionalInfo.transferNote: Max 50% of program; requires 80% curriculum match and $100 evaluation fee for external institutions.
additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Eligible to ladder into BBA - Accounting and Analytics with a minimum 2.0 GPA.

name: Bachelor of Business Administration - Accounting and Analytics
description: A four-year degree (or 2-year post-diploma) that advances students into complex financial decision-making, auditing, and corporate taxation. Prepares graduates for the Chartered Professional Accountant (CPA) designation.

tuition.note: Per year; rates vary by course load

domestic: Completion of a Business-related diploma (Accounting preferred)
international: Recognized Business diploma or 60 post-secondary credits
English language proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.0) OR TOEFL iBT 80
Minimum GPA equivalent of 2.0 on a 4.0 scale

additionalInfo.coopNote: Optional 4, 8, or 12-month paid work terms available after second year.
additionalInfo.specialNotes: Lays the groundwork for CPA Professional Education Program (PEP) entry.
additionalInfo.transferNote: Advanced standing available for graduates of accredited business diplomas.

name: Business Administration - Finance
description: A two-year diploma focusing on the fundamentals of sound financial management. Students investigate market trends, analyze economic factors, and master stock trading and corporate investing through hands-on case competitions.

tuition.note: Estimated for 2026/27; international rate based on $735/credit full course load.

international: High school diploma or international equivalent
English proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.5) OR Duolingo 115

additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Eligible to ladder into the BBA - Finance degree with a minimum 2.0 Graduation GPA.
additionalInfo.professionalDesignations: Foundation for CFP (Certified Financial Planner) and PRM (Professional Risk Manager).

name: Bachelor of Business Administration - Finance
description: A four-year degree designed for high-level financial decision-making. Covers advanced investment strategies, portfolio management, and risk analysis. Prepares students for senior leadership and professional designations.

tuition.note: Estimated per year for 2026/27; Year 2-4 international tuition is approx. $24,255 based on course selection.

highSchool: Overall minimum average of 65% in five required Grade 12 courses
postSecondary: Completion of a related Business diploma with minimum 2.0 GPA for Year 3 entry
Provisional entry available for GPAs between 1.7 and 2.0 via Open Studies pathway

additionalInfo.coopNote: Provides professional work experience with top-tier financial firms.
additionalInfo.specialNotes: Curriculum provides a strong foundation for the CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst) designation levels.

name: Civil Engineering Technology
description: A two-year diploma focusing on the design and maintenance of infrastructure like roads, bridges, and water systems. Includes hands-on training in CADD, structural design, and project management with an optional co-op stream.

tuition.note: Estimated per year for 2026/27 based on full course load; excludes books, supplies, and mandatory student fees.

international: High school diploma or international equivalent
English proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.5) OR Duolingo 115
Math and Physics equivalencies required (60% minimum)
Credential evaluation (e.g., IQAS or WES) may be required

additionalInfo.coopNote: Students apply after Semester 1; involves two 24-week work placements.
additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Graduates can ladder into NAIT's BTech (Management) or BTech (Construction Management). Transfer to UofA Engineering requires a competitive GPA (typically 3.5+).
additionalInfo.techRequirement: PC laptop required (approx. $2,000 cost) with specific GPU requirements for CAD software.

name: Mechanical Engineering Technology
description: A hands-on diploma focusing on the design, testing, and manufacturing of mechanical systems for industries like oil and gas and agriculture.

tuition.note: Estimated first-year tuition for international students is significantly higher due to lab-intensive requirements.

additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Graduates can ladder into NAIT's BTech (Management) or bridge to Lakehead University for a BEng degree.

name: Business Administration - Human Resource Management
description: Two-year diploma program combining classroom theory with hands-on case studies and simulations like mock interviews and mediation. Covers organizational change, employment law, and contract negotiations.

tuition.note: Estimated for 2026/27 academic year; excludes books and supplies

domestic: Math 30-1 or Math 30-2 or Applied Math 30 (60% minimum)
international: High school diploma or international equivalent
English proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.5) OR Duolingo 115

additionalInfo.professionalCredit: Accredited by CPHR Alberta; waiver of NKE exam with 70%+ GPA

name: Bachelor of Business Administration - Human Resources Management
description: Four-year degree (or two-year bridge for diploma holders) focusing on the strategic management of an organization's people. Includes advanced training in labor law, compensation strategy, and performance management.

tuition.note: Estimated per year for 2026/27; degree students typically pay per credit

domestic: Overall minimum average of 65% in five required Grade 12 courses
international: High school diploma with 65% average in core subjects
English proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 5.5) OR Duolingo 115
Business Diploma Pathway: 2.0 GPA in a related diploma for Year 3 entry

additionalInfo.professionalCredit: Foundational prep for CPHR designation; 3 years exp required vs 8 for diploma

name: Doctor of Pharmacy
description: Four-year professional program preparing students to become licensed pharmacists. Includes coursework in pharmaceutical sciences, patient care, and extensive practice experiences in clinical settings. Graduates are eligible to write the Pharmacist Evaluating Exam (PEBC) for licensure.

tuition.note: per year (verify exact amounts on tuition page)

prerequisites.note: Non-direct entry program. Requires 60 units (2 years) of university coursework before admission.
international: 60 units of university-level coursework (same courses as domestic)
English language proficiency: IELTS 7.0 (no band below 6.5) OR TOEFL iBT 100
Spoken Language Proficiency assessment may be required
All coursework must be from recognized post-secondary institutions

additionalInfo.specialNotes: Non-direct entry. Requires 2 years of prerequisite university courses before applying to PharmD program. Graduates eligible for PEBC licensure exam.

name: Nursing (year 1)
admissionLogic: Lottery-based (Fall 2026). Applicants with an 82% average in required courses are entered into a randomized selection pool.
description: The standard entry for high school students. A 3.25 to 3.5-year condensed program that includes foundational university studies in Year 1 before starting core clinical nursing in Year 2.

tuition.note: Per year estimate for 2026. Does not include textbooks or N-95 mask fit testing.

international: English proficiency: IELTS 7.0 (no band below 7.0) or TOEFL 92

name: Nursing (year 2)
admissionLogic: Lottery-based (Fall 2026). Applicants with a 3.00 GPA in required transfer courses are entered into a randomized selection pool.
description: An advanced entry route for students who have completed 30+ units of specific university-level prerequisites. Students bypass the foundational Year 1 and start directly in Year 2 clinicals.

tuition.note: Per year estimate for 2026. Adjusted for higher clinical course density.

name: Nursing (Medicine Hat)
institution: University of Calgary (Medicine Hat College Collaborative)
admissionLogic: Competitive Admission (Non-Lottery). Students apply to Medicine Hat College (MHC) for Year 1 & 2, then transfer to UCalgary status for Year 3 & 4 while staying in Medicine Hat.
description: A partnership program allowing students to earn a full University of Calgary BScN degree while completing all four years at the Medicine Hat College campus.

tuition.note: Tuition follows MHC rates for years 1-2 and UCalgary rates for years 3-4. Generally lower cost of living.

international: Standard international requirements for MHC/UCalgary partnership

additionalInfo.applicationDeadline: Check MHC website for specific local dates

name: Bachelor of Science in Software Engineering
description: A professional engineering degree focusing on the design, development, and maintenance of large-scale software systems. Part of the Schulich School of Engineering, this program emphasizes engineering principles, reliability, and security.

tuition.note: Per year; based on 2026 Board of Governors approved rates including Schulich-specific fees.

prerequisites.note: Admission is competitive based on five required 30-level subjects.
international: Senior secondary equivalent of English, Calculus, Chemistry, and Physics
English proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 6.0) OR TOEFL iBT 86

additionalInfo.specialNotes: Accredited engineering program leading to P.Eng designation. Includes a mandatory common first year.
additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Transfer students from SAIT may be eligible for individual course credit or admission to the Energy Engineering transfer program.
additionalInfo.transferNote: Check Transfer Alberta for course-by-course mappings. Minimum grade of C- required for transfer credit.

name: BSc in Computer Science (Software Engineering Concentration)
description: A Faculty of Science degree focused on the scientific and mathematical foundations of computing with a specialized concentration in software development processes, algorithms, and human-computer interaction.

tuition.note: Per year; estimated based on standard 2026 Faculty of Science rates.

prerequisites.note: Admission is competitive; Physics 30 is highly recommended for specific course prerequisites.
domestic: Two of: Biology 30, Chemistry 30, Physics 30, Math 31, or CTS Computing Science (Advanced)
international: High school diploma with senior-level English and Math
English proficiency: IELTS 6.5 (no band below 6.0) OR TOEFL iBT 86

additionalInfo.specialNotes: Features the Science Internship Program (8-16 months). More flexibility for electives compared to Engineering.
additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: UCalgary will move to block transfer for polytechnic diplomas starting in late 2026 (Fall 2027 intake).
additionalInfo.transferNote: Specific mappings for SAIT CPSC/MATH courses available via Transfer Alberta. Max 60 units of transfer credit allowed.

name: Certificate in Professional Management specializing in Construction Management
description: A 300-hour program developing competencies to effectively manage construction projects. Focuses on legal, regulatory, and technical aspects while building skills in business, cost estimation, and interpersonal relations.

tuition.note: Domestic includes $105 admission fee; International reflects the IPP full-time stream bundle.

prerequisites.note: No degree or diploma required for domestic admission; open to anyone 18+.
international: English proficiency: IELTS 6.5 or TOEFL iBT 86
Registration in the International Professional Program (IPP) stream

additionalInfo.totalProgramLength: Flexible based on courses per term
additionalInfo.specialNotes: Ideal for tradespeople moving into management. Students can take individual courses before formal admission.
additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Credits can transfer toward a Diploma in Professional Management.
additionalInfo.transferNote: Up to 100 hours of advanced standing may be granted for previous relevant post-secondary courses.

name: Bachelor of Commerce - Organizational Behaviour and Human Resources
description: A 4-year professional degree within the Haskayne School of Business. This program is the primary academic route for senior HR leadership and is fully integrated with ApplyAlberta for transcript automation.

tuition.note: Per year; 2026 undergraduate rates.

prerequisites.note: Transfer students require 18 units of 'Pre-Commerce' courses (English, Calc, Stats, Econ) with a C- minimum for entry.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Max 60 units (20 courses) allowed. ApplyAlberta handles all transcript requests from AB partner institutions automatically.
additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Standard 2+2 pathway for Business Administration diploma holders from any Alberta Transfer System institution.

name: Human Resource Generalist Diploma
description: A 275-hour professional diploma through Continuing Education. This is the 2026 replacement for the legacy HR certificate, specifically aligned with the CPHR Alberta competency framework.

tuition.note: Total program cost for domestic; International includes the IPP stream bundle.

prerequisites.note: Open entry; degree/diploma not required for domestic admission.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Up to 90 hours of 'Advanced Standing' can be granted for equivalent courses from other Alberta institutions.

name: Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering
description: Accredited engineering degree focusing on infrastructure, structures, and environment. Accepts diploma holders from any Alberta Engineering Technology program.

prerequisites.note: Diploma holders require a competitive GPA (typically 3.0+) for admission into 2nd year.

countryEquivalents.requirement: Senior High School Diploma (STEM strand). Must present high grades in Pre-Calculus, Calculus, and Physics.
Senior School Certificate. Requires 'B' grades or higher in Further Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry for competitive entry.
Senior High School Graduation Diploma + competitive Gaokao scores in the Science stream.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Sept 1, 2026 update: UCalgary now primarily grants block transfer credit (up to 30 units) for completed Alberta Engineering Technology diplomas.

name: Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering
description: Design and manufacturing of mechanical systems. Includes paths for Alberta polytechnic graduates via ApplyAlberta.

prerequisites.note: Requires completion of at least 8 specific 1st-year engineering courses for direct major entry.

countryEquivalents.requirement: Senior High School Diploma (STEM strand). Must present high grades in Pre-Calculus, Calculus, and Physics.
Senior School Certificate. Requires 'B' grades or higher in Further Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry for competitive entry.
Senior High School Graduation Diploma + competitive Gaokao scores in the Science stream.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Eligible for block transfer if transferring from an accredited Alberta Mechanical Engineering Technology diploma.

name: Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering
description: Study of power systems, electronics, and digital circuits. Direct transfer pathways exist for Alberta Electrical Engineering Tech grads.

prerequisites.note: English Language Proficiency must be met. Transfer GPA is calculated on the most recent 30 units of study.

countryEquivalents.requirement: Senior High School Diploma (STEM strand). Must present high grades in Pre-Calculus, Calculus, and Physics.
Senior School Certificate. Requires 'B' grades or higher in Further Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry for competitive entry.
Senior High School Graduation Diploma + competitive Gaokao scores in the Science stream.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Transcript retrieval via ApplyAlberta is automatic for students from member institutions.

name: Bachelor of Commerce in Finance
description: A competitive business degree within the Haskayne School of Business focusing on corporate finance and investments. Uses the ApplyAlberta system for seamless transcript transfer from any Alberta college.

tuition.note: Per year; estimated 2026 rates.

prerequisites.note: Transfer students must complete 6 specific 'Pre-Commerce' courses at an Alberta institution to be eligible for Year 2 or 3 entry.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Max 60 units (20 courses) allowed. ApplyAlberta automatically requests transcripts from participating AB institutions.
additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Accepts graduates from any accredited Alberta Business Administration diploma (e.g., SAIT, NAIT, Bow Valley, RDP).

name: Bachelor of Education (Elementary Route)
description: Professional teacher training for K-6. High-demand program that uses the Alberta Transfer System to evaluate non-education electives from colleges across the province.

prerequisites.note: Education major courses (EDUC) must be taken at UCalgary; they do not transfer from other colleges.

countryEquivalents.requirement: Must present senior-level English Literature (not just Language) and high marks in Science/Social Science strands.
Requires 'B' grades in 5 subjects, specifically including 'Literature-in-English'.
2 A-Levels and 3 GCSEs. At least one A-Level must be in a 'Teachable' subject (English, History, Math, or Science).

additionalInfo.specialNotes: As of 2026, UCalgary uses 'Block Transfer' for non-education credits (up to 30-60 units).
additionalInfo.transferNote: Transfer credit only applies to non-Education classes (options/electives).
additionalInfo.pathwayPrograms: Open to transfer students from all Alberta Transfer System (ATS) member institutions.

name: Civil Engineering Technology
description: 2-year accredited diploma specializing in infrastructure, structural design, and geomatics. Nationally recognized by Technology Accreditation Canada (TAC).

prerequisites.note: Accepts Math 30-2 stream. Competitive admission is not used; first-qualified applicants are accepted until full.

countryEquivalents.requirement: K-12 Diploma with strong marks in Pre-Calculus and Physics.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Eligible for 60 credits toward Athabasca University's Post-Diploma BSc.

name: Pre-Electrician
description: A 15-week certificate that provides the first year of the Alberta Apprenticeship curriculum. Prepares students to write the First Period Electrician exam.

prerequisites.note: Requires physical stamina and manual dexterity. High school diploma is the baseline.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Graduates earn credit for the first year of technical training in the Electrician trade.

name: Bachelor of Nursing (NESA)
description: A collaborative 4-year degree with the University of Lethbridge. The first two years are completed at the Polytechnic campus.

tuition.note: Years 1-2 rates; Years 3-4 paid to UofL.

prerequisites.note: Admission is highly competitive and granted by the University of Lethbridge.

countryEquivalents.requirement: Senior High Diploma with high marks in Biology and Chemistry.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Seamless transition to UofL for years 3 and 4.

name: Computer Information Technology
description: Focuses on software development, programming, and system analysis. Prepares students for the modern IT workforce.

prerequisites.note: Requires strong analytical thinking. PGWP eligible for international students.

additionalInfo.transferNote: CIPS accredited. Transfers to UofL Post-Diploma BSc in Computer Science.

name: Business Administration - Accounting
description: Two-year experiential diploma. Provides a foundation for the CPA designation. Focuses on financial reporting, taxation, and auditing.

prerequisites.note: English Language Proficiency must be met. Math 30-2 is the minimum requirement.

countryEquivalents.requirement: Senior High School Diploma with high marks in Business Math or Fundamentals of Accountancy.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Eligible for 60 credits toward Athabasca University's Post-Diploma BComm or B.Mgt at UofL.

name: Business Administration - Management (HR Focus)
description: Specialized management stream focusing on recruitment, leadership, and organizational behavior. Prepares students for CPHR pathways.

prerequisites.note: Includes an elective practicum component for real-world HR experience.

countryEquivalents.requirement: C6 or better in English and Mathematics.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Graduates can ladder into Athabasca's Post-Diploma Bachelor of Human Resources and Labour Relations.

name: University Transfer - Elementary Education
description: A 2-year university transfer pathway. Students complete their foundational 'breadth' courses in Lethbridge before transferring to a University for their B.Ed degree.

prerequisites.note: Transfer to University faculties of Education requires a high competitive GPA (typically 3.0+).
prerequisites.competetiveAverage: 75% - 80% (Admission to UT); 3.0+ GPA (For eventual University transfer)

countryEquivalents.requirement: Must include English Literature and core science/social subjects with 75% average.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Designed specifically for the 2+2 pathway into the University of Lethbridge or University of Alberta Faculty of Education.

name: General Arts and Science - Health Sciences
description: A 1-year foundational certificate. It provides the specific Biology, Chemistry, and Math prerequisites needed to transfer into professional Pharmacy or Pharmacy Technician programs.

prerequisites.note: Used as a 'ranking' or 'upgrade' year for students who don't yet meet Pharmacy degree entry requirements.

countryEquivalents.requirement: Senior High Diploma with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology credits.

additionalInfo.transferNote: Courses are designed for block transfer into Health Science degrees or as prerequisites for the U of A PharmD program.
